generations.label: F30
generations.common_competitors: Mercedes-Benz C-Class, Audi A4, Lexus IS

known_issues.title: Timing chain guide and tensioner failure (N20 engine)
known_issues.description: The N20 2.0L turbo four-cylinder is known for premature wear of plastic timing chain guides and the tensioner, which can allow the chain to skip and, in severe cases, cause catastrophic engine damage.
known_issues.symptoms: A rattling or clattering noise from the front of the engine on cold start-up, sometimes with the check engine light illuminating and a drop in engine power.
known_issues.recommendation: Ask for records showing the updated timing chain components were installed, and listen for a rattle on cold start-up.

known_issues.title: Oil filter housing gasket and valve cover leaks
known_issues.description: Plastic oil filter housing gaskets and valve cover gaskets degrade with heat cycling and begin seeping oil, often first noticed as a burning smell.
known_issues.symptoms: A faint burning oil smell after driving, along with visible oil residue on the top or front of the engine.
known_issues.recommendation: Check the top and front of the engine for oil residue and ask about recent gasket replacement.

known_issues.title: Electric water pump failure
known_issues.description: The electric water pump used on N20 and N55 engines has a known failure history, which can leave you stranded with a sudden overheat warning.
known_issues.symptoms: A sudden temperature warning light or overheat message on the instrument cluster, sometimes with no prior symptoms.
known_issues.recommendation: Ask for water pump replacement history and monitor the temperature gauge closely on the test drive.

known_issues.title: High-pressure fuel pump failure
known_issues.description: The direct-injection high-pressure fuel pump can fail, causing hard starts, misfires, or a no-start condition.
known_issues.symptoms: Difficulty starting the engine, especially when cold, along with occasional misfires or a complete no-start.
known_issues.recommendation: Have the car cold-started during your inspection to check for hesitation or difficulty starting.

known_issues.title: Aging plastic cooling system components
known_issues.description: Coolant expansion tanks, hoses, and connectors are made of plastic that becomes brittle with age and can crack, leading to slow coolant leaks.
known_issues.symptoms: A gradually dropping coolant level with white or crusty residue around hose connections and the expansion tank.
known_issues.recommendation: Check the coolant reservoir level and look for white residue around hose connections.
